name: Platform-Neutral Access Requirements
consequences: Implementing platform-neutral access requirements would directly reduce Denmark's dependence on Apple iOS and Google Android for digital identity services, mitigating the risk of supplier concentration. However, this might lead to increased complexity in system development and maintenance.
options: Embed platform-neutrality as a non-negotiable requirement in all future AltID procurements
Develop a standards-based, open-source alternative to MitID that can be deployed on any device or platform
Establish a certification program for digital identity apps that meet platform-neutral access standards
review: Controls supplier concentration vs. increased system complexity. Weakness: The options fail to consider the feasibility of implementing and enforcing strict platform-neutrality requirements across all future procurements.

name: Fallback Authentication Path
consequences: Implementing a fallback authentication path would provide an alternative means of authentication in case of platform failures or changes, enhancing resilience. However, this might increase costs and complexity.
options: Develop a browser-based or hardware-token-based fallback authentication system that can be used on any device
Implement a decentralized identity management system using blockchain technology
Create a standards-based API for fallback authentication that can be integrated into existing systems
review: Controls resilience vs. increased costs and complexity. Weakness: The options fail to consider the feasibility of implementing and integrating a fallback authentication path with existing digital identity systems.

name: Procurement Intervention
consequences: Influencing procurement processes would directly impact the development and deployment of digital identity services, potentially reducing dependence on foreign technology suppliers. However, this might lead to resistance from existing operators.
options: Develop a set of procurement guidelines that prioritize platform-neutrality and resilience
Establish a public-private partnership model for digital identity service development and deployment
Create a government-led initiative to promote the adoption of open-source and platform-neutral technologies
review: Controls supplier concentration vs. resistance from existing operators. Weakness: The options fail to consider the feasibility of influencing procurement processes and ensuring compliance with new guidelines.

name: Standards Pressure
consequences: Applying standards pressure would influence the development and deployment of digital identity services, potentially driving adoption of platform-neutral and resilient technologies. However, this might lead to increased complexity in system development and maintenance.
options: Develop a set of industry-wide standards for digital identity service development and deployment
Establish a certification program for digital identity apps that meet platform-neutral access standards
Create a public-private partnership model to promote the adoption of open-source and platform-neutral technologies
review: Controls complexity vs. increased complexity in system development and maintenance. Weakness: The options fail to consider the feasibility of implementing and enforcing industry-wide standards across all relevant stakeholders.

name: Credible Technical Demonstration
consequences: Developing a credible technical demonstration would provide evidence for the feasibility of platform-neutral and resilient digital identity services, potentially driving adoption. However, this might lead to increased costs and complexity.
options: Develop a non-production demonstrator that showcases a platform-neutral authentication path using open standards
Create a proof-of-concept prototype for a decentralized identity management system using blockchain technology
Establish a public-private partnership model to promote the adoption of open-source and platform-neutral technologies
review: Controls costs vs. increased complexity in system development and maintenance. Weakness: The options fail to consider the feasibility of developing and deploying a credible technical demonstration that meets industry standards.

name: Regulatory and Compliance Framework
consequences: Establishing a regulatory and compliance framework would provide a clear set of rules and guidelines for digital identity service development and deployment, potentially reducing dependence on foreign technology suppliers. However, this might lead to increased complexity in system development and maintenance.
options: Develop a set of industry-wide regulations for digital identity service development and deployment
Establish a certification program for digital identity apps that meet platform-neutral access standards
Create a public-private partnership model to promote the adoption of open-source and platform-neutral technologies
review: Controls complexity vs. increased complexity in system development and maintenance. Weakness: The options fail to consider the feasibility of implementing and enforcing industry-wide regulations across all relevant stakeholders.

name: Institutional Engagement
consequences: Engaging with institutional stakeholders would provide a clear understanding of their needs and priorities, potentially driving adoption of platform-neutral and resilient digital identity services. However, this might lead to increased complexity in system development and maintenance.
options: Establish a public-private partnership model to promote the adoption of open-source and platform-neutral technologies
Develop a set of industry-wide standards for digital identity service development and deployment
Create a certification program for digital identity apps that meet platform-neutral access standards
review: Controls complexity vs. increased complexity in system development and maintenance. Weakness: The options fail to consider the feasibility of engaging with institutional stakeholders and ensuring their buy-in.

name: EU-Level Engagement
consequences: Engaging at the EU level would provide a clear understanding of broader European requirements on interoperability, portability, resilience, and reduced platform lock-in. However, this might lead to increased complexity in system development and maintenance.
options: Establish a public-private partnership model to promote the adoption of open-source and platform-neutral technologies
Develop a set of industry-wide standards for digital identity service development and deployment
Create a certification program for digital identity apps that meet platform-neutral access standards
review: Controls complexity vs. increased complexity in system development and maintenance. Weakness: The options fail to consider the feasibility of engaging at the EU level and ensuring compliance with broader European requirements.

name: Interoperability Push
consequences: Direct effect: The Danish government pressures the EU to include platform-neutral access requirements in the European Digital Identity Wallet framework. Downstream implication: This could lead to a coordinated effort among member states to reduce dependence on Apple and Google, but might also face resistance from existing operators.
options: Drive EU-level policy changes through coalition building
Emphasize interoperability for critical public services
Develop a 'Digital Identity Passport' concept
review: Controls platform-neutral access vs. exclusive reliance on Apple/Google. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact of incumbent resistance from existing operators.

name: Public-Private Partnership
consequences: Direct effect: A public-private partnership is formed to develop and deploy a platform-neutral digital identity solution. Downstream implication: This might lead to increased investment in digital infrastructure, but could also raise concerns about data privacy and security.
options: Establish a joint venture between the Danish government and private sector companies
Develop a 'Digital Identity Hub' with multiple stakeholders
Create an open-source platform for digital identity
review: Controls public-private collaboration vs. exclusive reliance on government resources. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential risks of data sharing and security breaches.

name: Regulatory Incentives
consequences: Direct effect: The Danish government introduces regulatory incentives for companies to adopt platform-neutral digital identity solutions. Downstream implication: This could lead to increased adoption rates, but might also create a competitive advantage for larger companies.
options: Offer tax breaks or subsidies for companies using platform-neutral solutions
Create a 'Digital Identity Certification' program
Develop a 'Digital Identity Framework' for regulatory guidance
review: Controls regulatory incentives vs. market forces. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact on small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs).

name: Stakeholder Engagement
consequences: Direct effect: A broad coalition of stakeholders is engaged in the development and deployment of a platform-neutral digital identity solution. Downstream implication: This might lead to increased buy-in from various groups, but could also create conflicting interests.
options: Establish a 'Digital Identity Council' with representatives from government, industry, and civil society
Develop a 'Stakeholder Engagement Framework' for inclusive decision-making
Create an online platform for stakeholder feedback and discussion
review: Controls stakeholder engagement vs. exclusive reliance on government decisions. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential risks of conflicting interests and power imbalances.

name: Technical Demonstrators
consequences: Direct effect: Technical demonstrators are developed to showcase the feasibility of platform-neutral digital identity solutions. Downstream implication: This could lead to increased confidence in the technology, but might also create unrealistic expectations.
options: Develop a mobile-oriented demonstrator using open standards
Create a browser-based or hardware-token-based fallback authentication path
Design an 'Identity-as-a-Service' platform for scalability and flexibility
review: Controls technical demonstrators vs. focus on policy changes. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential risks of over-reliance on technology.

name: Procurement Reforms
consequences: Direct effect: Procurement reforms are introduced to favor platform-neutral digital identity solutions. Downstream implication: This could lead to increased adoption rates, but might also create a competitive advantage for larger companies.
options: Develop a 'Digital Identity Procurement Framework' for government agencies
Create a 'Procurement Incentives Program' for companies using platform-neutral solutions
Establish a 'Digital Identity Marketplace' for secure and transparent transactions
review: Controls procurement reforms vs. market forces. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact on SMEs.

name: Supply Chain Risk Profiling
consequences: Direct effect: Identify specific risks associated with relying on Apple iOS and Google Android for Danish digital identity access. Downstream implication: This profiling will inform procurement decisions, ensuring the selection of suppliers that align with Denmark's digital sovereignty goals.
options: Conduct a comprehensive risk assessment of current supplier concentration, focusing on potential security vulnerabilities and continuity-of-service exposure
Develop a detailed analysis of MitID's dependency on Apple/Google platform integrity signals, highlighting the consequences of vendor lock-in
Create a supply chain risk matrix to evaluate and prioritize mitigation strategies for identified risks
review: Controls supplier concentration vs. security vulnerabilities. Weakness: The options fail to consider the impact of existing relationships with Apple and Google on future procurement decisions.

name: Digital Identity Ecosystem Mapping
consequences: Direct effect: Visualize the complex relationships between MitID, AltID, and other digital identity stakeholders. Downstream implication: This mapping will facilitate a deeper understanding of the current ecosystem's dynamics, enabling more effective intervention strategies.
options: Develop an interactive map illustrating the various connections between MitID, AltID, and related digital identity services
Create a detailed graph showing the dependencies between different components of Denmark's digital identity infrastructure
Produce a visual representation of the relationships between key stakeholders in the Danish digital identity ecosystem
review: Controls digital identity landscape vs. stakeholder interactions. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential for mapping errors or outdated information.

name: Stakeholder Engagement Framework
consequences: Direct effect: Establish a structured approach to engaging with diverse stakeholders, including civil society organizations and government bodies. Downstream implication: This framework will facilitate more effective coalition-building and advocacy efforts.
options: Develop a stakeholder engagement plan that prioritizes building relationships with key organizations and individuals in the digital identity space
Create a matrix outlining the different communication channels and strategies for engaging with various stakeholders, including government officials and industry leaders
Establish a clear framework for evaluating the effectiveness of stakeholder engagement efforts
review: Controls stakeholder relationships vs. advocacy efforts. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential for over-reliance on existing networks or relationships.

name: Procurement Policy Reform
consequences: Direct effect: Influence procurement decisions to prioritize digital sovereignty and platform-neutral access requirements. Downstream implication: This reform will ensure that Denmark's digital identity infrastructure is built with resilience and continuity in mind.
options: Advocate for the inclusion of digital sovereignty and platform-neutrality language in official procurement documents
Develop a detailed analysis of current procurement practices, highlighting areas where reforms can be implemented to support Denmark's digital sovereignty goals
Create a proposal outlining a new procurement framework that prioritizes supplier diversity and resilience
review: Controls procurement policies vs. supplier relationships. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential for resistance from existing suppliers or industry leaders.

name: Regulatory Clarity
consequences: Direct effect: Provide clear guidance on regulatory requirements and standards for digital identity infrastructure in Denmark. Downstream implication: This clarity will ensure that industry leaders and stakeholders understand the expected standards of practice.
options: Develop a detailed analysis of current regulations and standards relevant to digital identity and sovereignty
Create a clear and concise guide outlining regulatory requirements and expectations for industry leaders and stakeholders
Establish relationships with key regulatory bodies, ensuring alignment between Denmark's advocacy efforts and EU-level policies
review: Controls regulatory environment vs. compliance. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential for regulatory ambiguity or conflicting priorities.

name: Institutional Overhaul
consequences: Direct effect: Reform institutional structures and processes to prioritize digital sovereignty and platform-neutral access requirements. Downstream implication: This overhaul will ensure that Denmark's institutions are equipped to support the country's digital sovereignty goals.
options: Develop a detailed analysis of current institutional structures and processes, highlighting areas where reforms can be implemented to support digital sovereignty
Create a proposal outlining a new institutional framework that prioritizes supplier diversity and resilience
Establish relationships with key stakeholders, including government officials and industry leaders, to ensure alignment between Denmark's advocacy efforts and EU-level policies
review: Controls institutional structures vs. policy influence. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential for resistance from existing institutions or industry leaders.

consequences: Direct effect: Establish clear standards and guidelines for digital identity infrastructure in Denmark. Downstream implication: This standardization will ensure that industry leaders and stakeholders understand the expected standards of practice.

Create a clear and concise guide outlining regulatory requirements and expectations for industry leaders and stakeholders
Establish relationships with key stakeholders, including government officials and industry leaders, to ensure alignment between Denmark's advocacy efforts and EU-level policies
review: Controls digital identity standards vs. compliance. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential for conflicting priorities or regulatory ambiguity.

consequences: Direct effect: Establish a clear framework for cybersecurity practices and protocols in Denmark's digital identity infrastructure. Downstream implication: This framework will ensure that industry leaders and stakeholders understand the expected standards of practice.

Create a clear and concise guide outlining regulatory requirements and expectations for industry leaders and stakeholders
